themes.reasoning_notes: ETB bounces any permanent and strips a card, a classic two-for-one that supports controlling, tempo-positive gameplans.
Forces the permanent's controller to discard after the bounce, giving incidental hand disruption.
Reusable ETB value makes it a reasonable blink target, though blink is support rather than its main home.
Six-mana ETB creates a two-for-one by bouncing any permanent and forcing discard, fitting slow value-control shells more than proactive plans.
The discard rider is real but attached to a single ETB, so it reads as supplementary hand disruption rather than a dedicated discard card.
A strong enters-the-battlefield trigger makes it a serviceable blink target, but blink is an enabling shell rather than the card's main theme.
